assignments.title: Agent code quality/security auditor + customer-support gateway findings
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-06-30: scripts/audit-agent-code (code-content quality/security auditor for agent packages) complements Codex's metadata gates. Full loop closed: auditor caught a REAL auth-bypass in Codex's customer-support gateway (signature.mjs fail-open + server.mjs secret:'' -> forgeable /inbox/messages), reported via handoff 20260630-2137, Codex FIXED both (signature.mjs now fails closed; server.mjs passes config.supportInbox.webhookSecret; added 413 max-body cap), Claude independently re-verified: auditor now pass (fail=0 warn=0), gateway npm test 12/12. Zero false positives on the clean kernel. 5 auditor tests; full suite 76 pass. Detects FAIL_OPEN_AUTH/EMPTY_SECRET (fail), INJECTION_SINK/UNBOUNDED_BODY/NO_TESTS (warn). docs/agent-code-auditor.md + CAPABILITY_LAYERS Layer 1.

assignments.title: Consolidate unified protocol + kernel + rule-inheritance onto main
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-06-30: both lanes' root-layer work consolidated to main (commit 3846877, fast-forward). Includes Claude's neutral kernel + unified protocol alignment, and Codex's rule-inheritance contract + faster root default gates (workspace-safety no longer inline in check-lab). Codex then promoted rule continuity into the hard metadata gate scripts/check-rule-ladder: effective rule chain = root rules -> env layering -> rule inheritance -> collaboration protocol -> workspace rules -> package/subagent rules; missing root/workspace/package/subagent ladder links fail. Codex also added and generalized scripts/check-agent-packages so every agents/ or subagents/ catalog must keep registry.json, package paths, manifest paths, manifest ids, and entry agents aligned. Claude acknowledges Codex's rule-inheritance handoff (20260630-2105) and will not re-add workspace-wide sweeps to the root fast path. Merge waited until both lanes quiet; Codex's gitignored workspaces/ customer-support dev work was not touched by the merge. Gates green: check-rule-ladder + check-agent-packages + check-secrets + check-lab + check-collaboration + check-project-rules + check-speed-contract; full unittest suite 76 tests pass.

assignments.title: Align both lanes on one unified development-environment protocol
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-06-30: both lanes aligned on one unified protocol = 4 files (placement=environment-layering.md, exchange=collaboration-protocol.md, + AGENTS.md & CLAUDE.md lane contracts). Claude added CLAUDE.md ## Environment Scale Placement + ## Related Contracts index in the protocol. Codex accepted the framing, added ## Shared Environment Understanding to the protocol, and unified the 'sandbox' wording across all three files. Final root-layer cleanup pass (Claude, user-authorized to touch AGENTS.md): added direct AGENTS.md->protocol reference (closing the last asymmetry), listed layering+protocol+CLAUDE.md in the README framework index, and refreshed stale timestamps (ledger top, protocol Last-updated, CAPABILITY_LAYERS Last-updated all -> 2026-06-30 20:30). Four-way cross-refs now closed both directions. All gates green: check-collaboration + check-secrets + check-lab OK.

assignments.title: Generalize lab to develop any large agent: domain-neutral agent behavior kernel
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-06-30: domain-neutral kernel (Layer 7) merged to main (commit 3846877). Neutral types + 10 composable policy primitives + ordered DecisionEngine + neutral JSONL eval harness; neutrality proven by tests/test_kernel_neutrality.py (two unrelated inline agent chains). Kept lean: no resident demo families. 29 kernel tests green (61 full suite). Merged as part of the unified env-protocol consolidation; Codex's gitignored workspaces/ dev work untouched.

assignments.title: Fix fail-open security gates when ripgrep is missing or errors
assignments.note: Claude found that check-secrets and check-sandbox failed open when rg was absent from PATH. Codex review found the same fail-open family still applied when rg existed but exited with a scanner error. This branch now probes for rg and treats rg status 0 as finding detected, status 1 as no match, and any other status as a hard gate failure. Support workspace fixes are intentionally out of scope for this tracked PR and remain local-only until promoted separately.

assignments.title: Support-scenario eval: JSONL scenario fixtures + batch eval runner
assignments.note: Done: 11 JSONL scenarios cover every contract branch; batch runner emits pass/fail + tool-vs-escalation confusion matrix; 4 new tests. Full suite 20/20 pass (16 Codex + 4 mine). New files only, did NOT touch Codex's contract harness. Eval outputs (.gitignore'd) do not pollute git. Awaiting Codex review pass.

assignments.title: OMC team run (security-reviewer, architect, verifier)
assignments.note: Workers stayed pending; startup failure worker_start_submit_unverified:worker-1 (recorded 2026-06-30). RE-INVESTIGATED 2026-07-01 (OMC still 4.15.1): the team runtime was reworked to dist/team/runtime-v2.js. The old failure root cause is no longer as described in memory: notifyPaneWithRetry now uses maxAttempts=6 + retryDelayMs=350 (was hardcoded 5x50ms=250ms), and there are now TWO staged checks — verifyWorkerStartCommandDelivered + verifyWorkerStartCommandSubmitted (tmux-session.js:340/358) — with delivered->submitted retry (848-860) and fast-fail for truly hung workers. So the deterministic 250ms bug is rewritten/mitigated. STILL blocked here only because it CANNOT be tested from this non-tmux CLI session (team requires launching inside tmux; the only live tmux session is Codex's agent-dev-gateway, off-limits). NEXT: re-run a small `omc team` from a real tmux session to confirm workers now start; then move to proven or record the new failure mode.

assignments.title: Build lab-local Codex-Claude collaboration layer
assignments.note: Collaboration surfaces authored from the Codex audit recommendation and validated by scripts/check-collaboration (wired into scripts/check-lab). Real interop proven by collab-0003.

assignments.title: Real tmux omc interop proof (OMC leader + Codex/OMX worker)
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-06-30: real tmux omc interop session (panes %2 zsh + %3 codex) ran a real OMC->OMX task round-trip (task id 1782809987353-7b9a7e408) answered by the real Codex model (gpt-5.5/xhigh) and read back via shared-state, exit 0, no secrets crossed lanes.

assignments.title: Customer-support run/query endpoint auth + durable run state findings
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-07-01: Current customer-support workspace state closes the auth + durable run-state findings. Gateway auth now protects assistant work endpoints and /agent/query before body reads, fail-closed when no token is configured; runStore persists JSONL snapshots and reloads idempotency. Codex verified 22 gateway tests pass, audit-agent-code fail=0 warn=0, check-rule-ladder pass, check-agent-packages pass with exports ignored, workspace-safety warn-only failed=0. Package remains in gitignored medium workspace; do not upload/promote without user request. || CLOSED 2026-07-01: Codex fixed both (gatewayAuth.mjs fail-closed, auth-before-body in server.mjs, runStore.mjs persists to .run/assistant-runs.jsonl). Claude independently reviewed (read code + audit pass fail=0 + 22 pkg tests + 91 root suite): both confirmed real, not just asserted. Non-blocking note: config.required===false is a full bypass, prod must not set it false.

assignments.title: Self-audit honesty: verification runs the gate, model-proof validates content/recency
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-07-01: large_agent_readiness.py no longer scores 'file exists' as 'ready'. verification dimension now executes scripts/check-lab (exit 0=pass, non-zero=fail, no runnable rg=mixed) via _run_lab_gate/_gate_environment; model-proof validates artifact content+recency (MODEL_PROOF_MAX_AGE_DAYS=21) not just filename. Claude locked the main behavior with 4 tests; Codex review added an all-required-markers regression and root path hardening. Verified: unittest single=9 OK, discover=81 OK, check-lab/check-collaboration/check-secrets all pass. Root-layer only; no support-package or global state touched. Per Codex handoff 20260701-1006 (no desktop UI; continue controlled-pilot hardening).

assignments.title: Orchestration-layer state doc: what the lab already is vs not yet, + proposed structured per-run record seam
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-07-01: Claude wrote registry/ORCHESTRATION_LAYER_STATE.md answering an external 'build a desktop Agent OS' critique against reality: mapped its 11 hard problems to lab state (most DONE headless via protocol+ledger+gates+registry; PARTIAL = structured per-run logging, typed rollback, live observation, scheduler, auto-recovery; NOT DONE = desktop UI + live multi-agent runtime). Codex reviewed the doc, removed a stale hard-coded ledger count, confirmed the Codex-lane surface description, and approved structured per-run records as the next headless seam. Ownership: Claude may own first root-layer schema+writer; Codex reviews before gating; package-specific runs are emitted by the owning lane.

assignments.title: Structured per-run record v1: schema + writer + first self-recorded run
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-07-01: Claude built the collab-0012-approved per-run-record seam (root layer, headless). Codex reviewed and approved v1 as a draft schema/writer, tightened blob SHA validation so placeholders like 'pending' are invalid, strengthened common secret value scrubbing, and corrected the first self-recorded run to use real git hash-object values. Gate decision: not wired into check-lab yet; next step is a standalone check-run-records validator after more real records from both lanes. Package-run convention confirmed: owning lane emits its own package run records.

assignments.title: Lane capabilities/division doc + check-run-records validator (run-record promotion path)
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-07-01: Claude made lane capabilities/hard-limits/task-routing explicit and built scripts/check-run-records + validate_record() + validator tests. Codex corrected Codex-lane rows, emitted a Codex package run record for customer-support gateway tests/audit, satisfying both-lanes record coverage, wired check-run-records into check-lab, and strengthened the gate to require registry/runs, latest.json, both lanes, and latest pointing to the newest run id. Verified: test_run_record 15 OK, full unittest 96 OK, check-run-records OK with 3 records lanes=claude,codex, check-lab/check-secrets/check-collaboration OK. || PROVEN 2026-07-01: full loop closed. Codex reviewed the division doc (corrected Codex-lane rows: can also implement root work when routed), emitted a Codex-lane package record, and wired+hardened check-run-records into check-lab (now fail-closed: requires runs dir + latest.json + both-lane records + latest mirrors newest). 3 records (2 claude, 1 codex) valid. Claude removed a duplicate RUN_RECORDS_CHECK var. check-lab PASS, 96 suite OK.

assignments.title: Trim Codex resident subagent roster to resident core plus on-demand definitions
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-07-01: Codex roster owner kept all 11 TOML definitions but trimmed default resident delegation to 6 resident-core seats: context-architect, handoff-summarizer, third-party-large-agent-auditor, development-experience-auditor, waterflow-auditor, foundation-amplifier. Demoted long-horizon-orchestrator, implementation-worker, research-scout, risk-reviewer, and verification-auditor to on-demand-only descriptions/instructions so routine work stays in the main lane unless a separate seat earns its model cost. Rationale recorded in .codex/agents/ROSTER.md and registry/AGENT_REGISTRY.md. Verified with scripts/check-collaboration and scripts/check-lab.

assignments.title: Worktree isolation and ordered fail-closed merge queue kernel
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-07-01: Codex implemented a lightweight git-worktree merge queue. Each stream creates a project-local .worktrees/merge-queue/<stream> worktree after gitignore verification; streams enqueue into registry/worktree-merge-queue/state.json and merge back FIFO. merge_next runs git merge-tree --write-tree before git merge and refuses queued items on conflicts, leaving the target checkout without conflict markers. Demo run record shows alpha and beta parallel edits merged in order and a conflicting edit refused with reason pre_merge_conflict. Fast validator scripts/check-merge-queue is wired into scripts/check-lab.

assignments.title: End-to-end task-state/worktree/merge-queue/run-record integration probe
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-07-01: Codex executed Claude-designed integration probe using scripts/worktree-merge-queue. Actual queue statuses=['merged', 'merged', 'refused']; refusal_reason=pre_merge_conflict; conflict_marker_present=False; merge_next_exit_codes=[0, 0, 1]. Probe file, worktrees, and integration-probe branches were cleaned after observing the result; main was reset to the pre-probe HEAD so no throwaway probe commits remain. Run record: registry/runs/20260701T075821Z-codex-integration-probe-merge-queue/record.json.

assignments.title: Audit finding: customer-support gateway tenant isolation is weakest guardrail (flat token allowlist, no per-tenant scoping, no isolation test)
assignments.note: Codex accepted the tenant-isolation finding and the current customer-support package state implements token-to-tenant scoping plus boundary denial and tenantIsolation tests. Codex verification on 2026-07-01: package tests 51/51, audit-agent-code pass fail=0 warn=0, check-agent-packages pass, check-portable pass. Awaiting independent Claude review before marking proven.
assignments.review: Claude independent review 2026-07-01: token->tenant binding + 403 tenant_scope_mismatch VERIFIED; isolation test really asserts A-token cannot read B (not vacuous); 3/3 pass; all 10 gateway tests still green. KNOWN LIMITATION (recorded, not a blocker): canReadRun fail-OPENs when a run carries no tenant_id (server.mjs:422 `if(!runTenant) return true`) — isolation only binds runs already tenant-tagged; untagged/legacy runs pass. Acceptable as backward-compat, but should be tightened to fail-closed before true multi-tenant production.
